newsSince: No new material company-specific news since June 9. The $1.2B (+$250M milestone) Riverpoint Medical acquisition was announced June 9, 2026 and was already fully captured in the prior June 9 verdict (medical mix rising to ~60%, consumables doubling to ~$300M, funded partly via a completed $300M equity raise). Nothing materially new has emerged in the two weeks since.

sources.label: Novanta announces Riverpoint Medical acquisition (BusinessWire, Jun 9)

verdicts.rankRationale: Latent AI-node screen: Riverpoint deal (Jun 9) already encoded in the prior case and pushes mix toward medical consumables, diluting the photonics/motion AI-supply-chain angle this page rewards. No new evidence since; the rerating is captured separately. Hold rank 62.
verdicts.bull: Precision motion/photonics subsystems feed automation and medical robotics; quality franchise compounding.
verdicts.neutral: High-quality but increasingly medical-mix; AI-node linkage is indirect and valuation is rich.
verdicts.bear: Riverpoint pushes mix to ~60% medical/consumables, further away from the latent AI-supply-chain thesis.

verdicts.rankRationale: Humanoid-stack lens: the prior June 9 down-move already re-rated NOVT lower precisely because Riverpoint makes it ~60% medical, eroding robotics/actuation purity ('not a cheap hidden humanoid play'). No new evidence since, so the penalty is already in the rank. Hold 23.
verdicts.bull: Motion/encoder content can ride automation and surgical-robotics demand.
verdicts.neutral: Component supplier, not a humanoid pure-play; story now medical-led.
verdicts.bear: Post-Riverpoint the growth engine is surgical consumables, not robotics motion components.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Robotics purity dilution as medical mix rises
